summary: Matrix Bio is developing small-molecule hyaluronidase inhibitors to increase high-molecular-weight hyaluronic acid (HMW-HA) in tissues, translating naked mole rat biology for cancer prevention/treatment and longevity. The document is a project overview template with key fields filled in, describing the therapeutic concept, mechanism, team, and IP strategy, but lacking specific experimental data or current development stage details.

content_blocks.title: Project Overview & Scientific Foundation
content_blocks.content: Project Title: Matrix Bio. Therapeutic Concept: Translate cancer-resistant and pro-longevity biology of naked mole rat (NMR) to humans by increasing HMW-HA in tissues using proprietary functional screening assay to discover small-molecule hyaluronidase inhibitors. Approach shifts balance toward HMW-HA and away from LMW-HA to suppress tumor progression and improve healthspan. Target Indication: Primary - Cancer prevention and treatment via tumor microenvironment modulation. Secondary - ECM/skin-related conditions (wound healing, arthritis, joint/skin conditions) and broader healthspan/longevity. Patient Population: Cancer caused ~10 million deaths and 19.3 million new cases worldwide in 2020.

content_blocks.title: Mechanism of Action
content_blocks.content: Small-molecule hyaluronidase inhibitors prevent enzymatic degradation of HA, increasing HMW-HA levels and reducing LMW-HA fragments. HMW-HA as seen in naked mole rat and NMR-HAS2 transgenic mice supports tissue homeostasis, suppresses tumor initiation and metastasis via CD44-mediated signaling, and is associated with improved healthspan and ~10% lifespan extension in mice.

content_blocks.title: Biological Target
content_blocks.content: Hyaluronidase enzymes responsible for HA breakdown (two prioritized targets, one undisclosed for IP reasons); indirectly targets the HA-CD44 axis and extracellular matrix composition.

content_blocks.title: Team Information
content_blocks.content: Key Personnel: Vera Gorbunova, PhD (Principal Investigator/Scientific Founder); VitaDAO/Longevity Dealflow Team for scientific/business evaluation and company formation (Sebastian Brunemeier, Tim Peterson, Rhys Anderson, Koen De Lombaert, etc.). Affiliations: University of Rochester, Rochester Aging Research Center, Rochester, NY, USA (Gorbunova Lab); Matrix Bio - newco to be launched jointly by VitaDAO and Gorbunova lab. Team Expertise: Comparative biology of aging and longevity, tumor suppression mechanisms, genome stability, extracellular matrix and hyaluronan biology, cancer biology, translational aging research, biotech/DeSci company formation and IP strategy.

key_findings: Therapeutic approach based on naked mole rat biology: increasing HMW-HA via hyaluronidase inhibition
Mechanism supported by NMR-HAS2 transgenic mouse data showing ~10% lifespan extension and tumor suppression
Target: hyaluronidase enzymes (two prioritized targets, one undisclosed for IP reasons)
Proprietary functional screening assay mentioned but no details on validation or hit compounds
Primary indication: cancer prevention/treatment; secondary: ECM/skin conditions and longevity
Large addressable market: 19.3M cancer cases, 10M deaths globally (2020)
Team led by Vera Gorbunova (University of Rochester) with VitaDAO support
Company formation stage: newco to be launched jointly by VitaDAO and Gorbunova lab
